payload.text: ai-system/project-control/README.md if it exists and needs an index/reference update

Add explicit Human Owner decisions for dependency satisfaction, reparenting, epic.key immutability and legacy TASK-XXX visibility.

Human Owner design decisions: hard dependencies are satisfied only by done; task reparenting is forbidden in MVP; epic.key is immutable after task refs exist; locks/resources are postponed unless required by TIG-05; legacy TASK-XXX remains resolvable during compatibility period.

payload.notes: TIG-02 reviewed and accepted. Epic key support implemented; EPIC-004 assigned TIG. Existing generated drift in CODEX_PROMPT.md and DOCS_GAPS.md accepted as pre-existing and not blocking this task.
TIG-03 reviewed and accepted. Task identity fields implemented with backward compatibility; resolver/dependencies/queue correctly deferred.
TIG-04 reviewed and accepted. Central task reference resolver implemented with TASK-XXX backward compatibility.

TIG-05 reviewed and accepted. Dependency graph, executable queue, dependency gates and generated TASK_EXECUTION_QUEUE.md implemented. Known DOCS_GAPS.md drift is pre-existing and not blocking this task.

payload.reason: TIG-06 migration depends on the dependency graph and executable queue implemented in TIG-05.

payload.notes: TIG-06 reviewed and accepted. Identity migration implemented, real project state migrated, EPIC-004 tasks mapped to TIG-01..TIG-06, generated outputs refreshed. Known DOCS_GAPS.md drift is pre-existing and not blocking this task.

ID allocation strategy depends on the current ctl inventory.

payload.notes: Inventory report accepted. Findings are sufficient for control-plane architecture design.
Unified control-plane architecture accepted. Design is sufficient for ID allocation strategy task.
Global ID allocation strategy accepted. Identity, ref, UID, alias, locking, validation, and parallel allocation rules are sufficient for core package design.
ai_project_ctl core package foundation accepted. Existing ctl behavior preserved; tests and validation passed.
Command registry foundation accepted. Registry is metadata-only, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.
Unified aictl facade accepted. Facade delegates to existing ctl scripts, registry discovery works, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.
Legacy ctl compatibility wrapper migration accepted. Shared plumbing extracted safely; domain behavior, command syntax, lifecycle gates, protected-file rules, and anti-recursion boundaries preserved.
Project doctor diagnostics accepted. PASS/WARN/FAIL model implemented; hard validation delegated to owning CLIs; stale prompt/context artifacts correctly reported as warnings.
Locking and atomic write protection accepted. FileLock, atomic writes, audit append fsync, tests, and compatibility checks passed.
